pattern: Because the city network is a tree, a subset of cities induces a connected subtree exactly when its internal road count equals one less than its size, which turns an exponential-looking subset search into a fast, checkable enumeration for small city counts.
hints: With at most 15 cities, every one of the 2^n possible subsets of cities can be examined directly.
A subset forms a connected subtree exactly when the number of original roads with both endpoints inside it equals (subset size - 1); anything else means it's disconnected.

For a connected subset, the maximum distance between any two of its cities (the diameter) can be found with two breadth-first searches restricted to that subset: BFS from any city to find the farthest one, then BFS again from that farthest city.

edge_inputs.description: four cities in a small branching tree, statement example
two cities joined by a single road, statement example
three cities in a straight line, statement example
maximum city count arranged as a single long chain, exercising every possible diameter value
a star-shaped network where leaf-only subsets (no hub) must be rejected as disconnected
a balanced branching tree where several different subsets share the same diameter
minimum city count, only one possible non-trivial subtree

stress.description: random attachment trees across the full allowed city-count range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, 15)
    edges = []
    for city in range(2, n + 1):
        parent = rng.randint(1, city - 1)
        edges.append([parent, city])
    return [n, edges]
